transcript: All right. Hey everyone. My name is Brandon, I am the co-founder and CEO of Phantom, and before I begin, I just want to say what an honor and privilege it's been to build on Solana and grow the ecosystem right beside everyone here in the room today. It's actually our fifth Solana Breakpoint this year and just about five years since we started Phantom originally and in that time, Solana's gone from underdog to undisputed biggest blockchain ecosystem in the world. I mean just look at this event today, right? And when we started to build Phantom originally, we chose to build on Solana because we wanted to build on the best foundation for the future of finance and that's never been more true than it is today. With Solana, it's finally possible for your financial life to be internet native. That means instant, global, programmable, and under your control. And Phantom is your financial account to do all of that. It brings you the tools and the connectivity to the ecosystem so that you can finally take control of your financial life. And just like all of the early adopters here, the next generation of consumers are also ready to take that step and bring their wallets as the center of their financial lives. So let's take a look at what we're shipping to make that happen. All right, so first, Phantom Cash. Phantom Cash is your daily money utility belt that lives in your pocket. You can spend, save, and earn frictionlessly across the globe. And we worked really closely with Stripe actually to bring this vision to reality, bring their money movement expertise to the Solana ecosystem, and together with them we developed the Cash stablecoin to power this experience. I'm proud to say that Cash actually passed a hundred million dollars of market cap recently and is actually the largest stablecoin that's built on top of Stripe's open issuance platform. This week, we're starting to roll out Phantom Cash to users across the globe, and we're going to have instant bank transfers, virtual accounts, and debit cards starting in the US. Now, let's take a look at what we're doing on the trading side. We're working hard to give users all the tools that they need to use crypto safely and easily, and that means supporting them throughout their trading journey every step of the way. Last month, we launched the Phantom Terminal on web, which brings pro-level trading into the same universe as your wallet. So with Phantom Terminal, you can spot trends early, perform powerful automations, and you can also stay synced on the go in mobile, and that's everything you need in order to be a successful trader in today's ecosystem. And actually speaking of mobile, not only on web, we've been delivering a lot of new trading experiences right on your phone as well. On mobile, we've launched the world's easiest on-the-go perps trading experience. Our goal is to make tools like perps more accessible, safer, and easier to use, and we're proud that Phantom is actually one of the top places to trade perps on mobile since we launched. But we're not focused just on consumers, we're building for developers as well, and developers are the heart of the Solana ecosystem. This week, we're officially opening up our new wallet platform called Phantom Connect. Phantom Connect is a free developer SDK for embedded wallets that brings all of the safety, the simplicity, and the ease of use that Phantom is known for directly into your app. Phantom Connect's killer feature are global wallets, so the days of having separate, isolated wallets for each one of your apps is a thing of the past. So why does this matter exactly? So if you're a builder, that means new users can onboard seamlessly without having to download another app. And if you're an existing Phantom user, you can sign into an app using your existing funds. And so for users, you have the same Phantom account, the same money, and the same identity that follows you wherever you go onchain, no matter what app that you're using. Funds aren't fragmented and the experience is simple and smooth. But that's not all. We have one more major announcement to make today. Today, I'm excited to announce that starting next week, we're rolling out prediction markets natively within Phantom in partnership with Kalshi and DFlow. And with this integration, Phantom is bringing three core innovations to the prediction market space. So one, trade prediction markets in a self-custody and directly onchain way. Two, chat with other traders live. Turns out prediction markets are a lot more fun when you're trading with your friends. And three, use any Solana token to trade. So no depositing required. So you can trade with SOL, USDC, Cash, or maybe even the leftover fart coin that you have from earlier this year. And with our integration, that's where the power of onchain interop really comes to life. And it's been an absolute pleasure to work with Kalshi on this integration. They've done a lot to make prediction markets safe and easy to use for a broader audience, so it was a very natural choice to work together with them to bring prediction markets to a broader crypto-native audience. So with that, I want to actually invite Kalshi's Head of Crypto, John Wang, onto the stage to talk a little bit more about the integration. Thanks Brandon, it's been a pleasure working with you guys. My name is John Wang, I'm the Head of Crypto at Kalshi. I think just like crypto 10 years ago, prediction markets are a net new asset class growing at the frontier of finance with supersonic speed. Having the largest crypto wallet, Phantom, be powered by Kalshi, which is the largest prediction market, will enable this category to reach a fresh new audience of crypto-native users. I think this integration will take prediction markets and Kalshi even further into the mainstream. Trading onchain events is now easier than ever thanks to the Phantom team. Thank you. Thanks John. All right. All right, so we announced a lot of new stuff today. We had prediction markets, Phantom Cash, and Phantom Connect. But how does this all fit together? When we think about growing the ecosystem, we think about this core flywheel loop right here. So by continuously lowering the barrier of entry for new users to get into the ecosystem, giving them a safe and easy set of tools to discover new things to do, like prediction markets, and giving developers the ability to tap into those users, we can all accelerate the adoption of Solana and crypto together to heights that we've never seen before. So in conclusion, Solana provides the internet's financial infrastructure. The community in this room are actually the ones that are building the cutting-edge applications, and Phantom is your internet-native financial account on top of it all. Everything great that's happened in the Solana ecosystem and that will happen is because of the people in this room. So huge shout out to the community and let's continue to accelerate. Thank you.

sources.title: Powering the next wave of digital asset utility with cards
sources.snippet:
Powering the next wave of digital asset utility with cards | Bridge
[
](https://www.bridge.xyz/)[
Request a demo
](https://www.bridge.xyz/requestfreedemo)
Menu
News
# Powering the next wave of digital asset utility with cards
By Ariana Poursartip
December 2, 2025
![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/66c78bffc753432b33c4f644/6927b4c5df98221716ec758f_Partnership%20post.jpg)
With Bridge's global card issuing platform[launch](https://usa.visa.com/about-visa/newsroom/press-releases.releaseId.21371.html)earlier this year, the barrier between holding stablecoins and spending them has disappeared. Developers can now offer their users the ability to pay at millions of merchants worldwide directly from their stablecoin balances, without friction, conversion delays, or the need to offramp to traditional banking.
‍For developers building on Bridge, card programs unlock new opportunities to enhance customer loyalty, boost engagement, and generate additional revenue through card interchange. The launch also compliments Stripe’s overall issuing capabilities, extending stablecoin-backed card programs for business audiences to consumer applications.
‍Since the launch, Bridge has been heads down listening closely to developers, expanding our offering, and helping customers launch in new markets. Today we’re thrilled to share a new wave of customers launching innovative spending solutions alongside new card issuing functionality.
‍## Innovators bringing more utility to stablecoins with card spend
‍From crypto wallets and remittance providers to neobanks, businesses are empowering their users with seamless ways to spend their digital assets.
‍Today Zepz, the global payments group behind WorldRemit and Sendwave serving 9 million customers, announced that Sendwave customers will soon be able to spend their stablecoin balances on everyday purchases through a Bridge-powered card. In October this year, Zepz announced the[Sendwave Wallet](https://zepz.io/press/zepz-launches-sendwave-wallet-to-give-customers-the-power-of-stablecoins-in-everyday-transactions/), enabling users to send, store, and spend USDC via a stablecoin-backed peer-to-peer cross border money solution supporting customers across 100 countries. Using Bridge’s smart contract functionality that links cards with their wallet infrastructure, Zepz can empower customers in multiple countries to compliantly spend stablecoin balances through one integration.
‍The card product, expected to go live in some markets including Brazil in early 2026, builds on Zepz’s mission to make financial empowerment simple, trusted and accessible for cross-border communities worldwide.
‍“For years, cross-border communities have been underserved by a financial system that wasn’t built for them. We’re changing that. By leveraging Bridge's infrastructure, Sendwave customers can use their digital dollar balance to not just send, receive and hold funds but also to spend them as an integral part of their daily lives. This isn’t just about moving money, but giving people better access, more stability and financial choice in their daily lives through a simple and trusted experience.”
Mark Lenhard, CEO of Zepz
Zepz
‍Phantom, a crypto wallet with more than 15 million users, is removing the friction around daily usage of crypto with[Phantom Cash](https://phantom.com/cash). Users can top up their balance through various funding methods, pay friends, and soon spend worldwide using a Bridge-powered card. Balances can be held and spent in CASH, a new stablecoin designed by Phantom and built on Bridge’s Open Issuance platform.h various funding methods, pay friends, and soon spend worldwide using a Bridge-powered card. Balances can be held and spent in CASH, a new stablecoin designed by Phantom and built on Bridge’s Open Issuance platform.
‍“Phantom Cash makes crypto feel as seamless and intuitive as money in your pocket. By powering these new features and card with Bridge and Stripe’s infrastructure, we’re giving our user

# Bringing Cross-Chain Wallets Into Aptos: X-Chain Accounts
[
![Aptos Labs](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fill:64:64/1*oFLHYpAFW3E9-ZMooaqahQ.jpeg)
](https://aptoslabs.medium.com/@aptoslabs?source=post_page---byline--b5ac60995143---------------------------------------)
[Aptos Labs](https://aptoslabs.medium.com/@aptoslabs?source=post_page---byline--b5ac60995143---------------------------------------)
3 min read
·Nov 5, 2025
[
](https://aptoslabs.medium.com/m/signin?actionUrl=https://medium.com/_/vote/aptoslabs/b5ac60995143&operation=register&redirect=https://medium.com/aptoslabs/bringing-cross-chain-wallets-into-aptos-x-chain-accounts-b5ac60995143&user=Aptos+Labs&userId=70211828fe2e&source=---header_actions--b5ac60995143---------------------clap_footer------------------)
--
[](https://aptoslabs.medium.com/m/signin?actionUrl=https://medium.com/_/bookmark/p/b5ac60995143&operation=register&redirect=https://medium.com/aptoslabs/bringing-cross-chain-wallets-into-aptos-x-chain-accounts-b5ac60995143&source=---header_actions--b5ac60995143---------------------bookmark_footer------------------)
Listen
Share
By Maayan Savir
Press enter or click to view image in full size
![]()
*TL;DR: Developers can now onboard Ethereum and Solana users directly into their Aptos dApps: no new wallets, seed phrases, or bridges required. With AIP-121 and AIP-122, Aptos natively supports authentication from external wallets like MetaMask and Phantom.*
The blockchain landscape has never been more fragmented. In 2025, more than $2 trillion in digital assets were distributed across over 40 active L1 and L2 networks. Yet every ecosystem remains a silo, each with its own wallets, key formats, and onboarding flows.
Over the past year, the Aptos ecosystem has expanded into a global trading engine, connecting DEXs, payments, and real-world assets into one high-throughput, low-latency network.
Accessibility is the next step for scale.
Thanks to AIP-113, Aptos can support native authentication standards from other ecosystems directly on-chain. Dev

editorial.headline: Phantom Evolves into Neo-Bank with Stripe-Backed 'CASH' and Native Kalshi Markets
editorial.standfirst: The wallet provider moves beyond simple custody, challenging traditional fintech with a $100M market cap stablecoin and seamless, regulated prediction markets.
key_takeaways: Phantom launches **CASH**, a yield-bearing stablecoin built on **Stripe's** Open Issuance platform, already exceeding $100M market cap.
New **Phantom Connect** SDK enables 'Global Wallets,' allowing users to carry identity and liquidity across apps without fragmentation.
**Kalshi** and **DFlow** integration brings regulated prediction markets directly in-wallet, supporting trades with any Solana token.
editorial.article_body_markdown:
Phantom is no longer just a wallet; it is effectively a bank that lives on the internet. In a defining keynote at Breakpoint 2025, CEO Brandon Millman unveiled a suite of products that aggressively target the traditional financial stack. The centerpiece is **Phantom Cash**, powered by the **CASH** stablecoin. Developed on **Stripe’s** Open Issuance platform (leveraging **Bridge** infrastructure), CASH has quietly amassed a $100 million market cap. Unlike isolated stablecoins, this integration offers genuine banking utility: instant transfers, virtual accounts, and Visa debit cards compatible with Apple Pay. By handling the 'last mile' of fiat-to-crypto friction, Phantom is positioning itself to capture the user's entire financial lifecycle, not just their speculative trades.

For developers, the launch of **Phantom Connect** represents a significant shift in the embedded wallet landscape. While competitors like Privy or Dynamic have focused on ease of onboarding, they often result in siloed 'app wallets' where funds are trapped. Phantom’s solution introduces the 'Global Wallet' concept—an embedded experience that retains a shared state. This allows users to sign into new applications using their existing Phantom identity and funds, solving the liquidity fragmentation that has plagued consumer crypto apps. It is a direct play to remain the liquidity layer of Solana, even as the interface layer fragments.

Perhaps the most immediate 'alpha' for investors is the native integration of **Kalshi** prediction markets. By partnering with **DFlow** for onchain execution, Phantom has bridged the gap between regulated US exchanges and DeFi liquidity. The killer feature here is capital efficiency: users can collateralize trades with *any* Solana asset—including SOL, USDC, or even memecoins—without needing to deposit into a separate exchange account. This unlocks billions in idle wallet assets for the prediction market economy, a sector that has already seen explosive growth in late 2025. 

The implication for the Solana ecosystem is clear: the infrastructure phase is maturing into a consumer fintech phase. Phantom’s pivot from a passive connector to an active financial platform—offering banking, trading, and betting under one roof—validates the thesis that Solana is the backend for the next generation of consumer finance. With **Stripe** handling the fiat rails and **Kalshi** handling the regulatory burden, Phantom is free to optimize the user experience, making 'living entirely onchain' a practical reality for its 20 million users.
